title: Microcharis medicaginea
taxonomy: Microcharis medicaginea belongs to the family Leguminosae, also known as the Fabaceae family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationship to other species within the family. Microcharis medicaginea is further classified as a species within the genus Microcharis. The genus Microcharis is itself part of the tribe Indigofereae. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary history and its position within the plant kingdom.

In traditional medicine, Microcharis medicaginea has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for medicinal use.
